verificationNote: AI-generated risk methodology aligned to ISO 27001:2022 and ISO 27005. Organizations should adapt to their specific context and risk appetite.
title: Information Security Risk Assessment Methodology
description: Structured methodology for assessing information security risks aligned to ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Clause 6.1 and ISO 27005 guidance.

levels.label: Rare
levels.description: May occur only in exceptional circumstances (<1% annual probability)

levels.label: Unlikely
levels.description: Could occur but not expected (1-10% annual probability)

levels.label: Possible
levels.description: Might occur at some time (10-50% annual probability)

levels.label: Likely
levels.description: Will probably occur in most circumstances (50-80% annual probability)

levels.label: Almost Certain
levels.description: Expected to occur in most circumstances (>80% annual probability)

levels.label: Insignificant
levels.description: Minimal impact on information security, no significant business disruption

levels.label: Minor
levels.description: Limited impact requiring internal response only

levels.label: Moderate
levels.description: Significant impact requiring management attention and potential notification

levels.label: Major
levels.description: Severe impact with regulatory reporting and significant business disruption

bands.label: High
bands.action: Treat within 30 days — escalate to ISMS owner

bands.label: Critical
bands.action: Immediate treatment — escalate to top management

reviewCycle: At least annually, or upon material change in risk context
